cede: verb. To give up; yield to another. [with to]
cedi: noun. The currency of Ghana, divided into 100 pesewas and represented by ₵.
cee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter C / c.
den: noun. A small cavern or hollow place in the side of a hill, or among rocks; especially, a cave used by a wild animal for shelter or
dene: noun. A valley, especially the deep valley of a stream or rivulet.
devein: verb. To remove the vein -like colon from (shrimp).
device: noun. Any piece of equipment made for a particular purpose, especially a mechanical or electrical one.
dice: noun. Gaming with one or more dice.
die: verb. To stop living; to become dead; to undergo death. followed by of as an indication of direct cause; general use:
diene: noun. An organic compound, especially a hydrocarbon, containing two double bonds.
din: noun. A loud noise; a cacophony or loud commotion.
dine: verb. To eat; to eat dinner or supper.
end: noun. The terminal point of something in space or time.
endive: noun. A leafy salad vegetable, Cichorium endivia, which is often confused with common chicory (Cichorium intybus).
envied: verb. simple past and past participle of envy
eve: noun. The day or night before, usually used for holidays, such as Christmas Eve.
evened: verb. simple past and past participle of even
evidence: noun. Facts or observations presented in support of an assertion.
evince: verb. To show or demonstrate clearly; to manifest.
evinced: verb. simple past and past participle of evince
nee: interjection. no, used to express no as a quantity, i.e. not any, like German kein /Dutch geen /French rien.
need: noun. A requirement for something; something needed.
niece: noun. A daughter of one’s sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either the daughter of one's brother (" fraternal niece "), or of
vee: noun. The name of the Latin script letter V / v.
vein: noun. A blood vessel that transports blood from the capillaries back to the heart.
veined: adjective. Having veins or veinlike markings.
vend: verb. Synonym of sell, to sell through a vending machine.
vendee: noun. The person to whom something is sold; a purchaser.
vice: noun. Bad or immoral behaviour. (Especially often, a habit that harms oneself or others)
vide: verb. Clipping of divide (to split or separate into two or more parts.).
vie: verb. To fight for superiority; to contend; to compete eagerly so as to gain something.
vied: verb. simple past and past participle of vie
vine: noun. Any plant whose stem requires support and which climbs by tendrils or twining.
vined: adjective. Having leaves like those of the vine
cid: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Chimariko.
cive: noun. Obsolete form of chive (“the herb”).
decene: noun. Any of many isomeric monoalkenes derived from a decane
deen: noun. religion; religiosity; the way of life of a pious Muslim
dei: interjection. Alternative spelling of dey (“informal term of address used when trying to get someone's attention.”).
deni: noun. A subdivision of currency, equal to 0.01 Macedonian denar.
devi: noun. A goddess in Vedic mythology, Hinduism and Buddhism.
eden: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Eden (“a paradise”).
evene: verb. To occur; to happen; to come to pass.
ide: noun. A freshwater fish of the family Cyprinidae, found across northern Europe and Asia, especially Leuciscus idus.
ned: noun. A person, usually a youth, of low social standing and education, a violent disposition and with a particular style of dress
nid: noun. Alternative form of nide (“nest of pheasants”).
vedic: adjective. Alternative letter-case form of Vedic.
